gazette: General Directorate of International Agreements and European Union
title: Procedures Regarding Newly Presented Electronic Origin and Circulation Documents
bodyHtml:
As per the 5th article of the Customs General Communiqué (International Agreements) (Serial No: 10), the list of countries that issue documents in electronic format is announced on the Ministry's official website.

However, there have been instances where despite not being listed in these announcements, certain countries have presented electronically issued origin/circulation documents. This has resulted in customs offices directly imposing additional duties and penalties.

The General Directorate of International Agreements and the European Union has issued a memorandum outlining the procedures to be followed in such cases.

The memorandum specifies the following steps to be taken when an electronically issued origin/circulation document is identified, even if it is not listed on the Ministry's website:

1.Subsequent verification of the document in accordance with relevant regulations.

2.If the verification results are negative, actions will be taken in accordance with Article 14 of the Communiqué.

3.If information is obtained from the authorized administration of the country where the document was electronically issued, prompt reporting to the General Directorate is required.

4.Following the General Directorate's review, instructions will be provided to the relevant Regional Directorate to conclude the process.

Please find an unofficial translation of the related letter of General Directorate of Turkish Customs below.


Republic of Turkey

Ministry of Trade

General Directorate of International Agreements and European Union


Ref: E-16934678-180.99-

Date: 20.10.2023 / 90156647


To Whom It May Concern,


As stipulated in the second paragraph of the 5th article of the Customs General Communiqué (International Agreements) (Serial No:10) published in the Official Gazette dated April 11, 2023, "The names of countries issuing documents in electronic format are announced on the Ministry's official internet site" based on the information received by our General Directorate. The names of these countries and the related documents are shared with the public in a list format on the official website of our Ministry.

However, particularly concerning certificates of origin, due to the lack of binding regulations in international law, there might be cases where foreign authorities have begun issuing documents in electronic format without notifying our Ministry.

Based on various inquiries received by our General Directorate, it has been observed that in instances where an electronically issued origin/circulation document, not listed on our Ministry's website, is presented, customs offices directly impose additional duties and penalties.

As explained above, it is probable that this discrepancy may not stem from misleading declarations by the taxpayer but rather from the failure of the exporting country to inform our Ministry.

Therefore, in order to prevent any potential grievances or public losses, in the event that an electronically issued origin/circulation document, not listed on our Ministry's website, is presented:

1.Subsequent verification of the document according to the relevant legislation is to be conducted.

2.In the event of a negative result in the subsequent verification, actions are to be taken according to the 14th article of the Communiqué.

3.If a response is obtained from the authorized administration of the country where the document was electronically issued, indicating that the document has commenced issuance in electronic format in their country, an immediate report to our General Directorate is required.

4.Following an examination by our General Directorate, instructions will be provided to the relevant Regional Directorate to conclude the subsequent verification process.

Please take the necessary actions based on this information.


Best Regards,


Atilla BASTIRMACI

Acting Director General


Distribution:

For Action: For Information:

All Customs and Foreign Trade Regional Directorates

Customs General Directorate
